channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australia’s Energy Future: Time to Move Away from Fossil Fuels and Embrace Renewables
item.description: As global tensions rise, particularly the ongoing war in the Middle East, Australia faces a critical opportunity to rethink its energy strategy. The war has sparked a new fuel crisis, pushing up petro...

item.title: Celebrating Indigenous Art and Identity at the Art Gallery of Western Australia
item.description: The I AM exhibition at the Art Gallery of Western Australia presents a rich and diverse collection of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander artworks, curated by Carly Lane, a Murri woman from Queensla...

item.title: eThekwini proposes tariff increases on essential services for 2026/27 financial year
item.description: The eThekwini Municipality has announced plans to implement significant tariff increases across several essential services, including water, electricity, sanitation, and refuse collection, as part of ...

item.title: How Banksy’s anonymity shaped the value and impact of his art
item.description: The recent report suggesting that Banksy is a 50-something man from Bristol, England, possibly named Robin Gunningham, has stirred debates across the art world. While some speculate that confirming hi...

item.title: Iran Allows Safe Passage for Non-Hostile Vessels in Strait of Hormuz Amid Tensions
item.description: Iran has officially informed the United Nations Security Council and the International Maritime Organization that non-hostile vessels can transit the Strait of Hormuz as long as they coordinate with I...

item.title: Arizona’s Role in the Sinaloa Cartel’s Gun Trafficking and the Ongoing Violence in Mexico
item.description: The ongoing conflict within Mexico's Sinaloa cartel, one of the country's most influential criminal groups, has been exacerbated by a surge in firearms trafficking from the United States, particularly...

item.title: Kenya Issues Flood Alert for Kisumu, Tana River, and Uasin Gishu Counties
item.description: The government of Kenya has issued a flood warning for communities situated along major rivers in western and coastal regions, as rising water levels have caused significant flooding. The Ministry of ...

item.title: Kenya Faces Fuel Supply Shortages Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Kenya’s fuel retailers are facing a major supply shortage as a result of the ongoing Iran conflict. According to Martin Chomba, the head of the Petroleum Outlets Association of Kenya (POAK), around 20...

item.title: YAS Enhances Environmental Conservation with Tree-Planting on Mount Kilimanjaro
item.description: YAS, a leading telecommunications company in Tanzania, has expanded its environmental conservation efforts with the “YAS Go Green for Kili” campaign. The initiative, which focuses on planting trees ar...

item.title: Kenya Seeks to Boost Trade with China to Address Trade Imbalance
item.description: Kenya is striving to expand trade with China to address a growing trade deficit that currently exceeds Sh500 billion. Deputy President Kithure Kindiki made this announcement during the High-Level Keny...

item.title: Spider-Man: Brand New Day Trailer Breaks Record with 718.6 Million Views in First 24 Hours
item.description: The official trailer for the upcoming Marvel and Sony Pictures film 'Spider-Man: Brand New Day' has shattered records by becoming the most viewed movie trailer in history within its first day of relea...

item.title: Artists' Unlikely Side Hustles: From Shoplifting to Mushroom Foraging
item.description: The article explores how some of the world's greatest artists supplemented their incomes through unconventional side hustles. Jean Genet, the celebrated French writer, perfected a unique method of ste...

item.title: Gujarat to set up India’s third satellite launch center near Gir Somnath
item.description: The Gujarat government has announced plans for India’s third satellite launch center, which will be located near Gir Somnath along the Arabian Sea. The site has been selected after consultations with ...

item.title: Omugo Community Condemns Abduction of Eight Worshippers at ECWA Church in Kwara, Calls for Urgent Rescue
item.description: The Omugo Development Union (ODU) don express deep sorrow and strong condemnation over the barbaric abduction of eight innocent worshippers at ECWA Church, Omugo in Oro Ago District of Ifelodun Local ...

item.title: The notwithstanding clause as protection against populism in Canada
item.description: In the debate over Quebec's Bill 21, which prohibits certain public sector workers from wearing religious symbols, legal experts have turned their attention to Canada's use of the notwithstanding clau...

item.title: House of Representatives Begins Impeachment Hearings for VP Sara Duterte
item.description: The House of Representatives on March 25, 2026, began the official hearings on the impeachment complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte. The hearing will cover various motions, subpoenas, and wi...

item.title: Rajasthan Villagers Protest to Protect Sacred Groves and Pastures from Large Solar Parks
item.description: In Rajasthan, the traditional oases of the desert known as orans, which are sacred groves managed by local communities, are under threat from large-scale solar power projects. These areas are crucial ...

item.title: Petrol Prices Soar Amid Fuel Supply Crisis and Global Tensions
item.description: Petrol prices in Australia have reached a record high average of $2.38 per litre, as the country faces a looming fuel supply crisis. According to reports, the crisis is set to worsen as Australian ref...

item.title: Local incident: Dog beaten to death, NGO volunteers face hostility while trying to help
item.description: In a disturbing incident reported recently, a man physically assaulted a dog, resulting in its death, in a local area. The attempt by NGO workers to intervene and save the animal was met with aggressi...

item.title: New Mexico Jury Orders Meta to Pay $375 Million for Violating Consumer Protection Laws Related to Child Safety
item.description: A New Mexico jury has ruled that Meta Platforms violated state consumer protection laws by misleading users about the safety of Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp, and by enabling child sexual exploita...
